Accurate and quantitative detection of impact force, especially at high values (100\u2013770 N), is challenging and often relies on electronic transduction and interfaces, with associated limitations in integration, shaping, and scalability. We present here a mechanochromic paint as a simple alternative for impact detection and visualization. The paint is based on the self\u2010assembly of polydiacetylene (PDA) and regenerated silk fibroin protein (SF) into core\u2010shell vesicles, which exhibit an irreversible mechanochromic transition from blue to red upon impact. The paint enables the tracking of the impact history on the applied material with a colorimetric response that is directly proportional to the impact energy across a broad sensing range of 100\u2013770 N (corresponding to 100\u20131000\u00a0MPa) and has wide utility given by its ability to be interfaced or integrated in a wide variety of material formats, as illustrated by through several demonstrator devices.<\/jats:p>","DOI":"10.1002\/advs.202518144","type":"journal-article","created":{"date-parts":[[2026,1,5]],"date-time":"2026-01-05T06:27:01Z","timestamp":1767594421000},"update-policy":"https:\/\/doi.org\/10.1002\/crossmark_policy","source":"Crossref","is-referenced-by-count":0,"title":["Visualizing
